english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod inexcusabilis es, o homo omnis qui judicas. In quo enim judicas alterum, teipsum condemnas: eadem enim agis quæ judicas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ ἀναπολόγητος εἶ ὦ ἄνθρωπε πᾶς ὁ κρίνων ἐν ᾧ γὰρ κρίνεις τὸν ἕτερον σεαυτὸν κατακρίνεις τὰ γὰρ αὐτὰ πράσσεις ὁ κρίνων
verses.king_james: Therefore thou art inexcusable, O man, whosoever thou art that judgest: for wherein thou judgest another, thou condemnest thyself; for thou that judgest doest the same things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scimus enim quoniam judicium Dei est secundum veritatem in eos qui talia agunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι τὸ κρίμα τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν κατὰ ἀλήθειαν ἐπὶ τοὺς τὰ τοιαῦτα πράσσοντας
verses.king_james: But we are sure that the judgment of God is according to truth against them which commit such things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Existimas autem hoc, o homo, qui judicas eos qui talia agunt, et facis ea, quia tu effugies judicium Dei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λογίζῃ δὲ τοῦτο ὦ ἄνθρωπε ὁ κρίνων τοὺς τὰ τοιαῦτα πράσσοντας καὶ ποιῶν αὐτά ὅτι σὺ ἐκφεύξῃ τὸ κρίμα τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And thinkest thou this, O man, that judgest them which do such things, and doest the same, that thou shalt escape the judgment of God?

verses.latin_vulgate: an divitias bonitatis ejus, et patientiæ, et longanimitatis contemnis? ignoras quoniam benignitas Dei ad pœnitentiam te adducit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ τοῦ πλούτου τῆς χρηστότητος αὐτοῦ καὶ τῆς ἀνοχῆς καὶ τῆς μακροθυμίας καταφρονεῖς ἀγνοῶν ὅτι τὸ χρηστὸν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰς μετάνοιάν σε ἄγει
verses.king_james: Or despisest thou the riches of his goodness and forbearance and longsuffering; not knowing that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance?

verses.latin_vulgate: Secundum autem duritiam tuam, et impœnitens cor, thesaurizas tibi iram in die iræ, et revelationis justi judicii Dei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ δὲ τὴν σκληρότητά σου καὶ ἀμετανόητον καρδίαν θησαυρίζεις σεαυτῷ ὀργὴν ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ὀργῆς καὶ ἀποκαλύψεως δικαιοκρισίας τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But after thy hardness and impenitent heart treasurest up unto thyself wrath against the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui reddet unicuique secundum opera ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ἀποδώσει ἑκάστῳ κατὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Who will render to every man according to his deeds:

verses.latin_vulgate: iis quidem qui secundum patientiam boni operis, gloriam, et honorem, et incorruptionem quærunt, vitam æternam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῖς μὲν καθ᾽ ὑπομονὴν ἔργου ἀγαθοῦ δόξαν καὶ τιμὴν καὶ ἀφθαρσίαν ζητοῦσιν ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: To them who by patient continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and immortality, eternal life:

verses.latin_vulgate: iis autem qui sunt ex contentione, et qui non acquiescunt veritati, credunt autem iniquitati, ira et indignatio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῖς δὲ ἐξ ἐριθείας καὶ ἀπειθοῦσιν μὲν τῇ ἀληθείᾳ πειθομένοις δὲ τῇ ἀδικίᾳ θυμός καὶ ὀργὴ
verses.king_james: But unto them that are contentious, and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, indignation and wrath,

verses.latin_vulgate: Tribulatio et angustia in omnem animam hominis operantis malum, Judæi primum, et Græci:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θλῖψις καὶ στενοχωρία ἐπὶ πᾶσαν ψυχὴν ἀνθρώπου τοῦ κατεργαζομένου τὸ κακόν ἰουδαίου τε πρῶτον καὶ ἕλληνος
verses.king_james: Tribulation and anguish, upon every soul of man that doeth evil, of the Jew first, and also of the Gentile;

verses.latin_vulgate: gloria autem, et honor, et pax omni operanti bonum, Judæo primum, et Græco:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δόξα δὲ καὶ τιμὴ καὶ εἰρήνη παντὶ τῷ ἐργαζομένῳ τὸ ἀγαθόν ἰουδαίῳ τε πρῶτον καὶ ἕλληνι
verses.king_james: But glory, honour, and peace, to every man that worketh good, to the Jew first, and also to the Gentile:

verses.latin_vulgate: non enim est acceptio personarum apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν προσωποληψία παρὰ τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: For there is no respect of persons with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quicumque enim sine lege peccaverunt, sine lege peribunt: et quicumque in lege peccaverunt, per legem judicabuntur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅσοι γὰρ ἀνόμως ἥμαρτον ἀνόμως καὶ ἀπολοῦνται καὶ ὅσοι ἐν νόμῳ ἥμαρτον διὰ νόμου κριθήσονται
verses.king_james: For as many as have sinned without law shall also perish without law: and as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law;

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim auditores legis justi sunt apud Deum, sed factores legis justificabuntur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ οἱ ἀκροαταὶ τοῦ νόμου δίκαιοι παρὰ τῷ θεῷ ἀλλ᾽ οἱ ποιηταὶ τοῦ νόμου δικαιωθήσονται
verses.king_james: (For not the hearers of the law *are* just before God, but the doers of the law shall be justified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem gentes, quæ legem non habent, naturaliter ea, quæ legis sunt, faciunt, ejusmodi legem non habentes, ipsi sibi sunt lex:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν γὰρ ἔθνη τὰ μὴ νόμον ἔχοντα φύσει τὰ τοῦ νόμου ποιῇ, οὗτοι νόμον μὴ ἔχοντες ἑαυτοῖς εἰσιν νόμος
verses.king_james: For when the Gentiles, which have not the law, do by nature the things contained in the law, these, having not the law, are a law unto themselves:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui ostendunt opus legis scriptum in cordibus suis, testimonium reddente illis conscientia ipsorum, et inter se invicem cogitationibus accusantibus, aut etiam defendentibus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινες ἐνδείκνυνται τὸ ἔργον τοῦ νόμου γραπτὸν ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν συμμαρτυρούσης αὐτῶν τῆς συνειδήσεως καὶ μεταξὺ ἀλλήλων τῶν λογισμῶν κατηγορούντων ἢ καὶ ἀπολογουμένων
verses.king_james: Which shew the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience also bearing witness, and *their* thoughts the mean while accusing or else excusing one another;)

verses.latin_vulgate: in die, cum judicabit Deus occulta hominum, secundum Evangelium meum per Jesum Christum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ὅτε κρίνει ὁ θεὸς τὰ κρυπτὰ τῶν ἀνθρώπων κατὰ τὸ εὐαγγέλιόν μου διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: In the day when God shall judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ according to my gospel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem tu Judæus cognominaris, et requiescis in lege, et gloriaris in Deo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: Ἴδε σὺ ἰουδαῖος ἐπονομάζῃ καὶ ἐπαναπαύῃ τῷ νόμῳ καὶ καυχᾶσαι ἐν θεῷ
verses.king_james: Behold, thou art called a Jew, and restest in the law, and makest thy boast of God,

verses.latin_vulgate: et nosti voluntatem ejus, et probas utiliora, instructus per legem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γινώσκεις τὸ θέλημα καὶ δοκιμάζεις τὰ διαφέροντα κατηχούμενος ἐκ τοῦ νόμου
verses.king_james: And knowest *his* will, and approvest the things that are more excellent, being instructed out of the law;

verses.latin_vulgate: confidis teipsum esse ducem cæcorum, lumen eorum qui in tenebris sunt,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέποιθάς τε σεαυτὸν ὁδηγὸν εἶναι τυφλῶν φῶς τῶν ἐν σκότει
verses.king_james: And art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind, a light of them which are in darkness,

verses.latin_vulgate: eruditorem insipientium, magistrum infantium, habentem formam scientiæ, et veritatis in lege.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παιδευτὴν ἀφρόνων διδάσκαλον νηπίων ἔχοντα τὴν μόρφωσιν τῆς γνώσεως καὶ τῆς ἀληθείας ἐν τῷ νόμῳ
verses.king_james: An instructor of the foolish, a teacher of babes, which hast the form of knowledge and of the truth in the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ergo alium doces, teipsum non doces: qui prædicas non furandum, furaris:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὖν διδάσκων ἕτερον σεαυτὸν οὐ διδάσκεις ὁ κηρύσσων μὴ κλέπτειν κλέπτεις
verses.king_james: Thou therefore which teachest another, teachest thou not thyself? thou that preachest a man should not steal, dost thou steal?

verses.latin_vulgate: qui dicis non mœchandum, mœcharis: qui abominaris idola, sacrilegium facis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ λέγων μὴ μοιχεύειν μοιχεύεις ὁ βδελυσσόμενος τὰ εἴδωλα ἱεροσυλεῖς
verses.king_james: Thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou that abhorrest idols, dost thou commit sacrilege?

verses.latin_vulgate: qui in lege gloriaris, per prævaricationem legis Deum inhonoras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ἐν νόμῳ καυχᾶσαι διὰ τῆς παραβάσεως τοῦ νόμου τὸν θεὸν ἀτιμάζεις
verses.king_james: Thou that makest thy boast of the law, through breaking the law dishonourest thou God?

verses.latin_vulgate: (Nomen enim Dei per vos blasphematur inter gentes, sicut scriptum est.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ γὰρ ὄνομα τοῦ θεοῦ δι᾽ ὑμᾶς βλασφημεῖται ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν καθὼς γέγραπται
verses.king_james: For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles through you, as it is written.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumcisio quidem prodest, si legem observes: si autem prævaricator legis sis, circumcisio tua præputium facta est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περιτομὴ μὲν γὰρ ὠφελεῖ ἐὰν νόμον πράσσῃς ἐὰν δὲ παραβάτης νόμου ᾖς ἡ περιτομή σου ἀκροβυστία γέγονεν
verses.king_james: For circumcision verily profiteth, if thou keep the law: but if thou be a breaker of the law, thy circumcision is made uncircumcision.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si igitur præputium justitias legis custodiat, nonne præputium illius in circumcisionem reputabitur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν οὖν ἡ ἀκροβυστία τὰ δικαιώματα τοῦ νόμου φυλάσσῃ οὐχί ἡ ἀκροβυστία αὐτοῦ εἰς περιτομὴν λογισθήσεται
verses.king_james: Therefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law, shall not his uncircumcision be counted for circumcision?

verses.latin_vulgate: et judicabit id quod ex natura est præputium, legem consummans, te, qui per litteram et circumcisionem prævaricator legis es?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κρινεῖ ἡ ἐκ φύσεως ἀκροβυστία τὸν νόμον τελοῦσα σὲ τὸν διὰ γράμματος καὶ περιτομῆς παραβάτην νόμου
verses.king_james: And shall not uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfil the law, judge thee, who by the letter and circumcision dost transgress the law?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim qui in manifesto, Judæus est: neque quæ in manifesto, in carne, est circumcisio:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ὁ ἐν τῷ φανερῷ ἰουδαῖός ἐστιν οὐδὲ ἡ ἐν τῷ φανερῷ ἐν σαρκὶ περιτομή
verses.king_james: For he is not a Jew, which is one outwardly; neither *is that* circumcision, which is outward in the flesh:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed qui in abscondito, Judæus est: et circumcisio cordis in spiritu, non littera: cujus laus non ex hominibus, sed ex Deo est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὁ ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ ἰουδαῖος καὶ περιτομὴ καρδίας ἐν πνεύματι οὐ γράμματι οὗ ὁ ἔπαινος οὐκ ἐξ ἀνθρώπων ἀλλ᾽ ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But he *is* a Jew, which is one inwardly; and circumcision *is that* of the heart, in the spirit, *and* not in the letter; whose praise *is* not of men, but of God.
